            "code": "# Pass 7 — Vein-burn inserts (Ch12–15) + Chloen agency adds (Ch9, Ch14)\r\n# Creates a new MD/DOCX and reports where each insertion landed (exact snippet + strategy).\r\n\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nimport re\r\nfrom docx import Document\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nfrom caas_jupyter_tools import display_dataframe_to_user\r\n\r\nsrc = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-PASS6-CadencePolish.md\")\r\ntext = src.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\nreport = []\r\n\r\ndef chapter_span(md_text, chap_num):\r\n    # Returns (start_index, end_index) for CHAPTER {num}\r\n    pat = re.compile(rf'(?im)^CHAPTER\\s+{chap_num}\\b.*$', re.MULTILINE)\r\n    m = pat.search(md_text)\r\n    if not m:\r\n        return None\r\n    start = m.start()\r\n    # find next chapter\r\n    next_pat = re.compile(r'(?im)^CHAPTER\\s+\\d+\\b.*$', re.MULTILINE)\r\n    n = next_pat.search(md_text, m.end())\r\n    end = n.start() if n else len(md_text)\r\n    return (start, end)\r\n\r\ndef insert_once(md_text, insert_text, at_index):\r\n    # Avoid duplicate if already present\r\n    if insert_text.strip() in md_text:\r\n        return md_text, \"skip-duplicate\"\r\n    return md_text[:at_index] + \"\\n\\n\" + insert_text.strip() + \"\\n\\n\" + md_text[at_index:], \"inserted\"\r\n\r\ndef place_after_keyword(md_text, span, keywords, insert_text):\r\n    s, e = span\r\n    block = md_text[s:e]\r\n    # Search keyword\r\n    for kw in keywords:\r\n        m = re.search(kw, block, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\r\n        if m:\r\n            # Place after paragraph containing keyword\r\n            # Find end of line/paragraph after match\r\n            para_end = block.find(\"\\n\\n\", m.end())\r\n            if para_end == -1:\r\n                para_end = len(block)\r\n            at = s + para_end\r\n            new_text, status = insert_once(md_text, insert_text, at)\r\n            return new_text, status, f\"after '{kw}' paragraph\"\r\n    # Fallback: after first paragraph of chapter\r\n    para_break = block.find(\"\\n\\n\")\r\n    if para_break == -1:\r\n        at = e\r\n    else:\r\n        at = s + para_break\r\n    new_text, status = insert_once(md_text, insert_text, at)\r\n    return new_text, status, \"fallback: after first paragraph\"\r\n\r\n# Insert definitions from the plan\r\nadd_ch9 = (\r\n    'Chloen took the chalk from his hand. \"I\\'m better at pictures,\" she said,\\n'\r\n    'and proved it. Her drawings were clumsy but true—the kind of true that\\n'\r\n    \"made men remember the mark when the page was gone. By late watch, she'd\\n\"\r\n    \"drawn three pages that looked like they'd been torn from the city's own ledger.\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nadd_ch14 = (\r\n    \"At the crowd's edge, Chloen moved between clusters with a bundle of hand-copied\\n\"\r\n    \"manuals. Her drawings—rough but honest—spread through hands faster than speech.\\n\"\r\n    \"By late glow, three crews had manuals they'd copied themselves, her pictures\\n\"\r\n    \"multiplying like chalk marks on a hot grid.\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nvb_ch12 = (\r\n    \"He checked the line before sleep. Four centimeters now—halfway to his hand.\\n\"\r\n    \"The discipline had cost him half a finger's width in a single day.\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nvb_ch13 = (\r\n    \"The copper-brown line sat at four centimeters, patient as law. It hadn't moved\\n\"\r\n    \"since yesterday. For now, stopping held.\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nvb_ch14 = (\r\n    \"He rolled his wrist and checked truth. Four and a quarter—the demonstration\\n\"\r\n    \"had taken more than he'd wanted to spend. He pulled the sleeve down and didn't\\n\"\r\n    \"make bargains with himself.\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nvb_ch15 = (\r\n    \"The line had stopped at four and a quarter centimeters. Not longer, but the\\n\"\r\n    \"copper-gold warmth in his chest sat thin as morning mist. He had coins left.\\n\"\r\n    \"Not many.\"\r\n)\r\n\r\n# 1) Ch9 agency insert\r\nspan9 = chapter_span(text, 9)\r\nif span9:\r\n    before = text\r\n    text, status, where = place_after_keyword(text, span9, [r'\\bmanual\\b', r'\\bfield\\s+manual\\b', r'\\bfield\\s+notes\\b', r'\\bledger\\b'], add_ch9)\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"9\",\"Type\":\"Chloen agency\",\"Status\":status,\"Placement\":where})\r\nelse:\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"9\",\"Type\":\"Chloen agency\",\"Status\":\"chapter-not-found\",\"Placement\":\"—\"})\r\n\r\n# 2) Ch14 agency insert\r\nspan14 = chapter_span(text, 14)\r\nif span14:\r\n    before = text\r\n    text, status, where = place_after_keyword(text, span14, [r'\\bdemonstration\\b', r'\\bcrowd\\b', r'\\bHeat[-\\s]?Rib\\s*3\\b', r'\\bpublic\\b'], add_ch14)\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"14\",\"Type\":\"Chloen agency\",\"Status\":status,\"Placement\":where})\r\nelse:\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"14\",\"Type\":\"Chloen agency\",\"Status\":\"chapter-not-found\",\"Placement\":\"—\"})\r\n\r\n# 3) Vein-burn inserts\r\n\r\n# Ch12: before chapter end\r\nspan12 = chapter_span(text, 12)\r\nif span12:\r\n    s,e = span12\r\n    # Insert right before end of chapter\r\n    text, status = insert_once(text, vb_ch12, e)\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"12\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":status,\"Placement\":\"end of chapter\"})\r\nelse:\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"12\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":\"chapter-not-found\",\"Placement\":\"—\"})\r\n\r\n# Ch13: early placement (after first paragraph)\r\nspan13 = chapter_span(text, 13)\r\nif span13:\r\n    s,e = span13\r\n    block = text[s:e]\r\n    para_break = block.find(\"\\n\\n\")\r\n    at = s + (para_break if para_break!=-1 else 0)\r\n    text, status = insert_once(text, vb_ch13, at)\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"13\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":status,\"Placement\":\"after first paragraph\"})\r\nelse:\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"13\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":\"chapter-not-found\",\"Placement\":\"—\"})\r\n\r\n# Ch14: after demonstration keyword if present, else before end\r\nif span14:\r\n    s,e = span14\r\n    block = text[s:e]\r\n    m = re.search(r'\\bdemonstration\\b', block, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\r\n    if m:\r\n        # insert after paragraph containing it\r\n        para_end = block.find(\"\\n\\n\", m.end())\r\n        if para_end==-1: para_end = len(block)\r\n        at = s + para_end\r\n        text, status = insert_once(text, vb_ch14, at)\r\n        place = \"after 'demonstration' paragraph\"\r\n    else:\r\n        text, status = insert_once(text, vb_ch14, e)\r\n        place = \"end of chapter (fallback)\"\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"14\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":status,\"Placement\":place})\r\n\r\n# Ch15: near end\r\nspan15 = chapter_span(text, 15)\r\nif span15:\r\n    s,e = span15\r\n    text, status = insert_once(text, vb_ch15, e)\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"15\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":status,\"Placement\":\"end of chapter\"})\r\nelse:\r\n    report.append({\"Chapter\":\"15\",\"Type\":\"Vein-burn\",\"Status\":\"chapter-not-found\",\"Placement\":\"—\"})\r\n\r\n# Save\r\nout_md = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-PASS7-Agency+VeinBurn.md\")\r\nout_md.write_text(text, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\ndoc = Document()\r\nfor line in text.split(\"\\n\"):\r\n    doc.add_paragraph(line)\r\nout_docx = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-PASS7-Agency+VeinBurn.docx\")\r\ndoc.save(str(out_docx))\r\n\r\n# Display report\r\ndf = pd.DataFrame(report)\r\ndisplay_dataframe_to_user(\"Pass 7 — Insertions Report\", df)\r\n\r\nprint(\"Updated manuscript:\", out_md)\r\nprint(\"DOCX:\", out_docx)",

    "03dbc5ab-4137-4c96-abaf-617262f6d6ea": {
      "children": [
        "f8c7fb20-552c-4b88-86f7-843eac124a60"

      "id": "03dbc5ab-4137-4c96-abaf-617262f6d6ea",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": "commentary",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "Found INTERLUDE I line: Interlude  \tI: Perfect and Pure \nInterlude  \tI: Perfect and Pure \n\t●  \t  \t \t \n\tCorlexi  \t \t— Perfect Lines \n\t●  \t \t \n\tMobel   \t— Cleansing Fire \n \t \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t6: Teaching Rhythm \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t7: Parallel Lines \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t8: City Grammar \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t9: Field Manual \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t10: Under Load \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t11: Fracture Edge \n\t●  \t \n\tInterlude  \tII: Rumor & Report \n\t●  \t  \t \t \n\tSalee   \t— Everyone’s Pain \n\t●  \t \t \n\tEries   \t— Endings Are Natural \n \t \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t12: Drift Align \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t13: Morning-Of \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t14: Weight Test \n\t●  \t \n\tChapter  \t15: Set True \nChapter 1: Something Wrong \nKindling light seeped down the Underface in a thin wash, barely enough to make the chalk marks shine. Jhace tasted limestone dust and old heat. The grid hummed under his boots—good tone, steady, a watch’s worth since the last check. He ran his thumb along a riser seam anyway, feeling for the small betrayals: a grit line, a warmth that wasn’t earned, a whisper where there should be a throat-sung note. \n“Torque key,” he said. \nChloen already had it out, two spans of oiled steel, teeth still sharp. She moved like a tally of nerves wrapped in a hand of resolve. Bram stood back with the posture of a man pretending to be at rest, copper bracer scuffed to the bone under its patina. He pretended a lot. Never about the work. \n“Saddle pin’s shy a quarter-turn,” Jhace said. “Hear it?” \nChloen cocked her head. “I hear hum.” \n“Hum’s not the song,” Bram said. “What’s the song, girl?” \nChloen squinted, tongue pinned against a molar as if that might wring sound from steel. The grid breathed through the riser—soft, regular, three heads of pressure on the gauge window, fogged at the edges. A good note rose in the forearm of pipe that crossed the catwalk, then sagged just a fin\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1763206014.4611824,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "03dbc5ab-4137-4c96-abaf-617262f6d6ea",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# Inspect the Interlude I section headings to find exact strings.\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nfrom docx import Document\r\nimport re\r\n\r\nbase_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds.docx\")\r\ndoc = Document(str(base_path))\r\nfull_text = \"\\n\".join(p.text for p in doc.paragraphs)\r\n\r\n# Find Interlude I start index\r\nmatch_interlude = re.search(r\"INTERLUDE\\s*I.*\", full_text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\r\nprint(\"Found INTERLUDE I line:\", match_interlude.group(0)[:120] if match_interlude else \"NOT FOUND\")\r\n\r\n# Show 1200 chars after Interlude I to inspect headings\r\nif match_interlude:\r\n    start = match_interlude.start()\r\n    snippet = full_text[start:start+1800]\r\n    print(snippet)",

        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "### INTERLUDE II: RUMOR & REPORT\n\n#### PART A: KEIR — THE ROPE\n\nKeir heard the failure before the city admitted it.\n\nThe bell rope in his hands rasped like a bad conscience. Heat-Spine low-noted underfoot. Proof-wall lip on the Outer Crown coughed load three times in a row like it was trying to hide blood. He didn’t have a pattern for that, so he made one: short, short, hold. The roofs answered a beat late, then in time. Good. Grammar still worked.\n\nDust walked in a straight column along the gate. Too steady for wind. Sola took the flags without waiting—white crossed with amber, then red cut narrow—her hands quicker than her fear. Down in the street, a cart tried to"
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1763141025.285429,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "05f6fea3-193e-44f6-8329-5491065e19ba",

            "code": "# Insert the Corlexi horror block after the sentence \"Hands lacked principles. He worked around them.\"\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nfrom docx import Document\r\nimport re\r\n\r\nbase = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds.docx\")\r\ndoc = Document(str(base))\r\nfull = \"\\n\".join(p.text for p in doc.paragraphs)\r\n\r\n# Locate Corlexi section boundaries\r\nm_start = list(re.finditer(r\"Corlexi\\s*—\\s*Perfect\\s+Lines\", full))[-1]\r\nstart_idx = m_start.start()\r\n\r\nm_end = re.search(r\"Mobel\\s*—\\s*Cleansing\\s+Fire\", full[start_idx:])\r\nassert m_end, \"Could not find 'Mobel — Cleansing Fire' after Corlexi section.\"\r\nend_idx = start_idx + m_end.start()\r\n\r\nsection = full[start_idx:end_idx]\r\n\r\nanchor = \"Hands lacked principles. He worked around them.\"\r\ninsert_block = (\r\n    \"Meve will still be useful, he reminded himself, and looked for the fault he could remove.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Adren’s hands shook as he lifted the next length. The tremor traveled into the truss and made a small, ragged song.\\n\"\r\n    \"“Still,” Corlexi said. The word did not arrive as instruction. It arrived as fact.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Blue-silver gathered across his knuckles, thin as wire. He pressed two fingers to Adren’s forearm and drew a line the way he drew it in steel—along grain, not across it. \"\r\n    \"The boy’s tremor flattened. The arm held with a borrowed geometry that did not belong to meat.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Adren sucked a breath. “Master, that hurts.”\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"“Pain is a measurement,” Corlexi said. “It tells us where the slack was.”\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Meve watched and did not speak. The lattice sang cleaner. The crew stood taller as if someone had subtracted weight from the day and added it somewhere else.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Adren tried to flex. The wrist did not answer like a wrist. It answered like a brace. Corlexi approved. The work would hold better now.\\n\\n\"\r\n)\r\n\r\nassert anchor in section, \"Anchor sentence not found in Corlexi section.\"\r\nsection_updated = section.replace(anchor, anchor + \"\\n\\n\" + insert_block, 1)\r\n\r\n# Rebuild full text\r\nnew_full = full[:start_idx] + section_updated + full[end_idx:]\r\n\r\n# Save DOCX and MD\r\nnew_doc = Document()\r\nfor line in new_full.split(\"\\n\"):\r\n    new_doc.add_paragraph(line)\r\n\r\nout_docx = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REV-InterludeI-Corlexi.docx\")\r\nnew_doc.save(str(out_docx))\r\n\r\nout_md = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REV-InterludeI-Corlexi.md\")\r\nout_md.write_text(new_full, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\nprint(\"Inserted Corlexi horror passage in Interlude I.\")\r\nprint(\"Saved:\", out_docx)\r\nprint(\"Saved:\", out_md)",

    "09e27394-4152-4311-a318-1b5f3360ce04": {
      "children": [
        "b591828c-e2fb-40f5-9218-6f79af1f42e2"
      ],
      "id": "09e27394-4152-4311-a318-1b5f3360ce04",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "### CHAPTER 12: DRIFT ALIGN\n\nThe forge had been turned back into a room that could stand to be quiet. The floor-map was a ghost under fresh sweep-chalk; the anvil held a stack of stamped slips pretending to be authority; the rafters kept the day’s heat like a promise it might break later.\n\nJhace sat at the table with a stub of charcoal and the manual’s first page. His right hand shook in a fine, irritating buzz. The copper-brown line inside his wrist—new length, finger-joint exact—felt cool the way water feels cold when your teeth already hurt. He set his forearm on the wood to steal steadiness from it. The letters behaved.\n\n“Marks,” Tiffani said, pulling a chair with the care a person reserves for something she plans to use again. She had ink and a straightedge, a bottle with a mean stopper, and a small jar of oil that made her knuckles loosen a hair when she looked at it. “X-in-circle first. Then double-bar. Then arrows.” She met his eyes to make sure he’d heard. “Then the page on stopping.”\n\nHe huffed something like a laugh. “Thought I’d lead with ‘spend until collapse.’”\n\n“You tried that,” she said, and her mouth did a precise, unfriendly thing that still counted as affection. “It lacks elegance.”\n\nHe drew. X with the circle sung tight around it; his tremor made the first line a hair off. He lifted, reset, drew through the shake. The symbol read true enough. He wrote, in a blocky hand that had learned dignity from rivet tags: **done, checked**. Beneath it: **double-bar = checked under load**. He wanted a better word than load because the city was under something larger, but load was the word that bought obedience.\n\nTiffani copied clean beside him, her ink lines firm as spine. Blue-silver did not light at her knuckles; she didn’t spend for penwork. She did flex her left thumb twice before the bottle’s cork surrendered, and rubbed a sting of oil into the base of the joint like a person filing a burr off a tool. Cost: logged.\n\n“Page two,” she said. “Arrows. Where warmth invites. Where alignment starts.”\n\n“Small arrows,” he said, and drew them smaller than pride would want, so crews wouldn’t see a target big enough to lie to.\n\n“Page three,” she said, eyes on him rather than the paper. “Stopping.”\n\nHe looked at his wrist because honesty counted even when no one else was in the room. “Stopping,” he said, and the word tasted like a bolt you’d over-tightened and were trying to back off without snapping.\n\nShe reached across and set the flat of her hand next to his, not touching, parallel as rails. “We need three cues,” she said. “My call. Your feel. The mark.”\n\nHe nodded, listening to the idea like you listen to a plate. “Your call: **Stop. Coins.**”\n\n“My call,” she confirmed. “Your feel?”\n\n“The buzz goes saw-tooth,” he said. “Vision shifts at the edge like heat haze. The third note gets… persuasive.”\n\nShe wrote it without flinching at how ugly it looked in ink. “And the mark?”\n\nHe turned his wrist. The line sat there like a rule. “When it moves, we stop. Not negotiate. Stop.”\n\n“Good,” she said, and made it a line item, ordinary to the point of reverence. “We teach crews to point at your wrist before they ask you for anything.”\n\n“Bram already does,” he said.\n\n“Bram uses smaller words,” she said, and let the corner of her mouth relax the smallest breath.\n\nHe drew the last arrow and set the stub down. The buzz in his fingers kept going the way kettles keep talking after you lift them off heat. He anchored his hand with the other and watched the tremor notch down slowly, like a clutch finding.\n\n“You saved me,” he said.\n\n“Copy,” she said. No ceremony.\n\n“I didn’t know your name,” he said, and found there was space in his chest to say it in clean air. “For a breath. I looked at you and knew… the angle, the weight, the way your shoulder sits when you’re about to lie to a plate. But the word was gone.”\n\n“That’s what it does,” she said, precise even with fear. “I don’t care what we call it. We don’t let it do it again.”\n\nHe nodded. A bell far down-crown muttered in a grammar he didn’t need to answer yet. The forge door was cracked to let not-wind in; sounds slipped under it like shy cats—carriers, a distant argument turning into instructions, the captain’s voice somewhere making work behave.\n\nOn the table, the pages began to look like a language. Tiffani’s neat blocks; his blocky script; diagrams that would make sense to hands too tired for paragraphs. He wrote: **Coins, not pours**. Then underlined it, not twice, but with a line firm enough to be heard by men who ignored softer lines.\n\n“Show me,” she said after a time.\n\nHe looked up. “What.”\n\n“How you stop when it wants you,” she said, and lifted her chin toward the small bracket they’d left on the table for tests. “At three breaths. I’ll call.”\n\nHe hated and loved her for that. He set his palms near the seam. Copper-gold rose, obedient, small as a coin in a poor man’s pocket. The bracket answered, easing toward itself.\n\n“One,” she said.\n\nHe kept it tight. The buzz in his fingers sharpened to a more honest annoyance. The third note showed up like an unwelcome auditor and stood at his shoulder.\n\n“Two,” she said.\n\nHe felt his chest open the wrong way, the door wanting to throw itself wide. He held it on the hinge.\n\n“Stop,” she said.\n\nHe stopped. The warmth cooled against his will and also because he wanted it to. The itch to pour walked up his jaw and tried to make him clench; he breathed it into a corner and left it there.\n\n“Again,” she said.\n\nThey did it once more, and once more, until the call and the feel met like clap and echo. Until stopping felt like the end of a sentence rather than a denial.\n\nHe took his hands back and flexed. The filament hadn’t moved; the buzz had. He smiled, small and real. “Better.”\n\n“Good,” she said. She put oil on her thumb again and didn’t bother to hide the wince this time. “I hate this part.”\n\n“The oil?” he asked.\n\n“The admitting,” she said. “That there’s a part I can’t precision my way past. That I use my hands like a tool I don’t want replaced and the tool lets me know it wants replacing.”\n\n“They’re your hands,” he said. “They’ll look different. They’ll still obey.”\n\n“Useful,” she said, and the word meant more than praise.\n\nThey went back to letters because letters were a way to rest without lying down. He wrote a little note in the margin next to **Stop**: **if the hum turns you into a rope, cut it**. He wasn’t sure if any man would understand that sentence. He knew she would.\n\nThe room slowed. The bell lied about rest and they didn’t answer, but they let the bell take some of the weight of deciding. The lantern threw a shape on the table that made their two shadows meet and pass on the page.\n\n“Let me see your wrist,” she said, and he gave it. She set her fingertips near, not touching, reading the mark the way a glassworker reads a ripple. “Three,” she said, to the line, as if it could be bargained with by being named, and then, quieter, “No farther.”\n\nHe heard the sentence like a clamp taking hold. “Copy.”\n\nHe should have stood then. He didn’t, because the room had begun to feel like a safe angle. He looked at her hands in the mean light—ink smudge, oil shine, tendons standing like cable under light skin—and at the way she reached to cap the bottle and the thumb caught, and the tiny exhale she didn’t want noticed.\n\n“You don’t have to hold everything,” he said back to her, to prove he’d learned English in this new dialect.\n\n“That’s what you’re here for,” she said, the corners of her mouth bending toward something not quite allowed.\n\nHe moved his hand. Not to touch her first. To put it near enough that she could decide to make the span into a join. She looked at the offered place, precise as a surveyor, and made the measurement.\n\nThis was not public work. No audience. No performance. The kiss was a small mark drawn clean, not generous, set where it would carry the most for the least spend. The tremor in his fingers steadied to nothing for the space of it. The cold at her thumb let go one degree. It made a sound in his chest like a hanger seating. It sat.\n\nThey didn’t chase it. They did what they’d been teaching all day. They stopped while it could still be read.\n\nHe breathed. The city breathed back, almost in time. Outside, someone laughed two streets away and then turned the laugh into a direction for a crew. A draft found the door and filed dust off the floor-map’s ghost.\n\n“Tomorrow,” she said, forehead resting for one second against his, a practicality, not drama, like checking a level. “We take these pages to the gate office.”\n\n“Make pictures loud,” he said.\n\n“And if the proof-master sneers,” she said, “you will not pour just to wipe the expression off his face.”\n\nHe snorted. “We’ll invite.”\n\n“We’ll invite,” she echoed.\n\nShe stood. He stood. The buzz tried to return and found fewer places to live. He rolled his sleeve down over the neat, unarguable line and felt the word **stop** attach to it like a label that might hold.\n\nThey lay down within reach of tools, a brace of bodies set where load wanted them. When the bell lied, they pretended belief. When the city asked again—and it would—they would answer. Not with a pour. With a page, a mark, a breath, a hand placed where it did the most for the least. And, when they forgot, they would remind each other where to stop."
          ]
        },

          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# Pass 1 trims for Chapters 1–3 on the current manuscript version (with Interlude I upgrade).\r\n# We'll work from the MD produced in the last step, then export updated MD + DOCX and a change log.\r\n\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nimport re\r\nfrom docx import Document\r\n\r\nbase_md = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REV-InterludeI-Corlexi.md\")\r\nassert base_md.exists(), \"Base MD not found. Please ensure the Interlude I upgrade file exists.\"\r\n\r\ntext = base_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\n# Helper: extract chapter blocks by heading\r\ndef extract_block(name, content):\r\n    # Match from heading line to next \"CHAPTER \" or \"INTERLUDE\" or end.\r\n    m = re.search(rf\"^(CHAPTER\\s+{name}:[\\s\\S]*?)(?=^CHAPTER\\s+\\d+:|^INTERLUDE\\s|^$)\", content, flags=re.MULTILINE)\r\n    return m\r\n\r\nchanges = []\r\n\r\ndef replace_in_block(content, block_span, replacements):\r\n    start, end = block_span.span(1)\r\n    block = content[start:end]\r\n    original = block\r\n    for label, pat, repl in replacements:\r\n        new_block, n = re.subn(pat, repl, block, flags=re.DOTALL)\r\n        if n:\r\n            block = new_block\r\n            changes.append(f\"[{label}] Replaced {n} occurrence(s) of pattern: {pat[:60]} -> {repl[:60]}\")\r\n    return content[:start] + block + content[end:], original, block\r\n\r\n# --- Chapter 1 trims ---\r\nm1 = extract_block(\"1\", text)\r\nassert m1, \"Chapter 1 not found\"\r\nreplacements_ch1 = [\r\n    # Remove the copper taste / pennies meta aside entirely in Ch1 (per canon placement)\r\n    (\"Ch1 copper/pennies removal\",\r\n     r\"“?It tastes wrong,”\\s*Jhace said\\.\\s*“?Like old pennies\\.”\\s*Then laughed at himself because pennies weren’t a thing here, just a story someone’s grandfather had told about some other city, some other casting\\.\",\r\n     r\"\"),\r\n    # Also remove any leftover single-line 'Like old pennies' if phrasing differs\r\n    (\"Ch1 stray pennies\", r\"“?Like old pennies\\.”\", r\"\"),\r\n    # Tighten Underface/bone sentence\r\n    (\"Ch1 Underface bone trim\",\r\n     r\"The Underface carried the city’s heat the way bone bears weight: mostly without complaint, until it suddenly complained too much\\.\",\r\n     r\"The Underface carried the city’s heat the way bone bears weight: mostly without complaint.\"),\r\n]\r\ntext, ch1_before, ch1_after = replace_in_block(text, m1, replacements_ch1)\r\n\r\n# --- Chapter 2 trims (light/no-op unless obvious clutter) ---\r\nm2 = extract_block(\"2\", text)\r\nassert m2, \"Chapter 2 not found\"\r\nreplacements_ch2 = [\r\n    # Keep this pass minimal; example trim: drop the generic pretty/true aphorism to reduce tell\r\n    (\"Ch2 minor trim pretty/true\", r\"Pretty rarely corresponded to true\\.\\s*\", r\"\"),\r\n]\r\ntext, ch2_before, ch2_after = replace_in_block(text, m2, replacements_ch2)\r\n\r\n# --- Chapter 3 trims ---\r\nm3 = extract_block(\"3\", text)\r\nassert m3, \"Chapter 3 not found\"\r\nreplacements_ch3 = [\r\n    # Remove redundant standalone \"He ran.\" (we retain the detailed sequence that follows)\r\n    (\"Ch3 remove 'He ran.' line\", r\"^\\s*He ran\\.\\s*$\", r\"\",),\r\n    # Trim the second sentence of the 'embarrassing to have feared' couplet\r\n    (\"Ch3 trim indulgent aside\", r\"\\s*Humans loved to feel foolish in exchange for not dying\\.\", r\"\"),\r\n    # Remove \"almost pretty\" line to keep tone severe during catastrophe\r\n    (\"Ch3 remove 'almost pretty'\", r\"\\s*It was almost pretty if you pretended you weren’t in the middle of it\\.\", r\"\")\r\n]\r\ntext, ch3_before, ch3_after = replace_in_block(text, m3, replacements_ch3)\r\n\r\n# Save outputs\r\nout_md = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-PASS1-Trim-Ch1-3.md\")\r\nout_md.write_text(text, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\n# Build DOCX from updated text\r\ndoc = Document()\r\nfor line in text.split(\"\\n\"):\r\n    doc.add_paragraph(line)\r\nout_docx = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-PASS1-Trim-Ch1-3.docx\")\r\ndoc.save(str(out_docx))\r\n\r\n# Create change log with before/after diffs (simple excerpt-based)\r\ndef excerpt(label, before, after):\r\n    return f\"### {label}\\n\\n**Before (excerpt):**\\n\\n{before[:800]}\\n\\n**After (excerpt):**\\n\\n{after[:800]}\\n\"\r\n\r\nlog = \"# Pass 1 Trims — Chapters 1–3\\n\\n\"\r\nlog += \"## Summary of Changes\\n\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(f\"- {c}\" for c in changes) + \"\\n\\n\"\r\nlog += excerpt(\"Chapter 1\", ch1_before, ch1_after)\r\nlog += excerpt(\"Chapter 2\", ch2_before, ch2_after)\r\nlog += excerpt(\"Chapter 3\", ch3_before, ch3_after)\r\n\r\nlog_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Pass1-Trims-Ch1-3-Log.md\")\r\nlog_path.write_text(log, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\nprint(\"Saved updated manuscript and change log:\")\r\nprint(\"MD:\", out_md)\r\nprint(\"DOCX:\", out_docx)\r\nprint(\"Log:\", log_path)",
            "end_time": 1763206432.8791645,

    "0e73372e-4562-49c7-8ef4-42908f1f6b48": {
      "children": [
        "04a29724-5bb3-4f40-9d21-3b3165916c75"
      ],
      "id": "0e73372e-4562-49c7-8ef4-42908f1f6b48",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "### CHAPTER 14: WEIGHT TEST\n\nMorning watch put a thousand small intentions on Heat-Rib 3. Ropes already tied. Bells hung head-high with chalk under them: eyes, hands, back. The captain walked the catwalk like a level held true. Two hundred people breathed together and made the steel listen.\n\nTiffani flexed. The fingers didn’t. Oil had become theater; the joint at the base of her left thumb held a cold argument and refused to be persuaded by anything but rest she wasn’t going to give it. Blue-silver stayed quiet at her knuckles. She could lay marks. She could speak the work. She could not promise the tiny, exact nudges that made a hanger confess its last rattle. Not at this watch.\n\nJhace set himself in her periphery like a brace that moved when she did. He checked the bell-notes under the signs—eyes, hands, back—touched the chalk, rolled his wrist under the sleeve like a man confirming a truth he’d decided not to fight. His right hand buzzed a small, irritating hum. The copper-brown line at his inner wrist sat finger-joint long and precise. It hadn’t advanced in the night. For now that was a kind of mercy.\n\n“Crowd’s bigger than we wanted,” Bram said, voice all hinge and comfort.\n\n“We wanted none,” she said, measuring the load path with her chin. “We brought two hands of runners. It’ll be enough.”\n\n“It will if you keep them from trying to help,” the captain said, not smiling. “Teach by doing. Then do.”\n\nTiffani nodded and drew the first marks loud: X-in-circle at yesterday’s proofed join; double-bar at the hinge they’d seat under eyes; a square for **align first**; two small arrows for **invite warmth here**. The crowd leaned without stepping—a good student’s posture.\n\nHer fingers caught when she capped the ink. She pretended not to feel it. “Sequence,” she told Jhace, and he stepped close enough that the city could have called them one person without being wrong.\n\n“Your line,” he said, calm as a plate not yet asked to move. “My coin under it. On your breath. We stop when you say.”\n\n“That’s the whole plan,” she said.\n\n“It’s a good plan,” he said.\n\nHis steadiness was a measurable thing. She put the flat of her hand against his chest, set her forehead to his, and made their breathing match for three breaths. On the third, his tremor smoothed to something a body could ignore. The crowd quieted by contagion. She took her hand back and the plan was real.\n\n“Lift,” the captain called to her crew, “not pry. On her breath.” She jerked her chin at Tiffani. “You’re the metronome.”\n\nTiffani set her palm near the seam and felt the angle that wanted to exist. Precision didn’t require a perfect hand; it required a perfect refusal. She shaped refusal with her whole body—the way her shoulder sat, the way her jaw did not clench, the way her breath made a line.\n\n“One,” she said, which meant nothing mathematical and everything practical.\n\nJhace put a coin of warmth under the edge she was turning. Copper-gold rose like a held promise. The hinge considered the argument. Bram minded the quarter-turn but did not spend it.\n\n“Two,” she said, and the crew lifted, not pried. The hum shifted, a fraction truer.\n\n“Stop,” she said.\n\nHe stopped. The coin cooled because she told it to and because he wanted it to. The third note pressed his bones from the inside like a finger testing a bruise; he didn’t let it write.\n\n“Again,” she said, and the second pass seated. The hinge gave a finger of truth. A wallman drew X-in-circle with a hand steadier for having work.\n\n“Down-line,” the captain said, thin grin now, “for the fools who didn’t see the first one.”\n\nThey walked three plates and did it again, then two more. Teaching happened out loud—runners repeating the names of marks, captains translating breath to motion—without her having to shout. She felt the crowd learn: a softening across shoulders, a thousand small muscles choosing not to brace for the wrong thing.\n\nHer thumb bit when she adjusted a square a hair left. Cold ache pulsed hard enough to make her vision try to gray at the edges. She filed the pain where it belonged: noted, not obeyed. “Arrows,” she said, and handed the chalk to a kid with a bell-runner scarf who drew them like he’d been born wanting to. “Good,” she said, and meant the word like a stamp.\n\nThey reached the hinge that would not be polite.\n\nA yaw in the seat. A history of wrong quarter-turns. Load that had learned to wander. The catwalk betrayed it with a faint chatter under boots, a talkative plate somewhere that wanted to be the center of the room. The crowd felt the wrongness and leaned back without needing to be told.\n\nTiffani mapped the corrections by knuckle. “Two upstream,” she said to the captain, “one bleed down-line. Then we teach this hinge without giving it ideas.”\n\nJhace’s sleeve shifted as he rolled it—habit, ledger—then settled back down. “On your breath,” he said. “Small coins.”\n\n“Smaller than you like,” she said. It came out kinder than she felt.\n\n“Copy.”\n\nThey set. She marked **align first**—square and arrow—then laid her palm near the seam. Jhace fed a coin the size of honesty. The plate eased by a hair. Bram took a quarter when the hum was ready, not before. The hinge grudged. The chatter kept talking.\n\n“Less,” she said.\n\nHe did less. The coin barely there, exactly there. The hinge stopped sulking by the width of a hair.\n\n“Breathe,” she told the crew, and they remembered how.\n\nHer hand failed her at the worst possible moment. The base of her thumb seized and the line she was drawing went crooked in her own body. He felt it before she swore and set a second coin under her failing edge—not generous, just enough. She hated him for a breath. Then she accepted the help. Precision wasn’t loneliness. Not anymore.\n\n“Now,” she said, and Bram turned a quarter like a bell timing thunder. The hinge gave.\n\nSomething moved in the crowd—a ripple, not feet, attention tilting away. Two men with clean cuffs and patient smiles had been watching since Kindling, the particular stillness of people who believed ownership came by observation. They’d brought a scribe with a board and a quill that drank too much ink.\n\n“Make it safe, then go home,” the scribe said, loud enough to be heard. “We’ll take it from here.”\n\nThe captain didn’t turn. “No,” she said, and made the word weigh a stone.\n\n“We have lists,” the scribe said, as if that were the same as jurisdiction.\n\n“Put my name on top,” Bram said, friendly as an anvil, and the crowd laughed in a way that put the scribe outside the circle where work mattered. The men held their smiles like shields and stayed. That was fine. Tiffani did not waste precision on faces.\n\n“Again,” she said, to the hinge.\n\nThey worked it by halves until it said yes in the right language. The wallman drew the double-bar thick as a vow. Bells above them carried the grammar down-line—one for **eyes**, two for **hands**, three for **back**—and the path rearranged itself to their needs.\n\nBetween jobs, Tiffani’s fingers refused to close around the chalk. She clicked the piece to the deck and nudged it to Jhace with the back of her hand. “You draw,” she said. “I’ll call.”\n\nHe crouched, scar-shine catching pale light, and made her marks in his blocky hand—**align first**, arrows, X-in-circle—listening to her while his warmth stayed where it belonged. She watched the neat copper line inside his wrist and the way his sleeve wanted to ride up to show it. It hadn’t moved. Yet. She would keep it that way if stubbornness could be spent like coin.\n\n“Hands,” the captain called to the crowd, pointing at Tiffani’s stiff fingers so they’d see the cost and not build a superstition. “She’s paying for this same as you pay your rent. Mind your corners. Don’t make her spend where you can carry.”\n\nThe crowd nodded. Pride changed shape in their faces—less idol, more partnership. Good. That would save a life by nightfall.\n\nLate watch slanted across steel. They were two plates from the crest when the rib decided to be honest. A buckle a half-bar down—outside their demonstration circle, inside the circle of consequence—rolled its shoulder and tried to start a collapse the way men start fights when they think you won’t bother to answer.\n\n“Weight test,” the captain said, calm as clamps. “Show them.”\n\nTiffani set her palm and her stance and made refusal even with a traitor joint. “On my breath,” she told everyone, not just the crew. “Coins.”\n\nJhace set the smallest warmth under the edge she named. The buckle hesitated. Bram made a quarter-turn when the hum allowed. The buckle thought about being a story and decided against it.\n\n“Stop,” she said, and he did, instantly. The watching crews saw the stopping as the lesson, not just the making. Good. That, too, would save a life.\n\nThe men with the quill leaned together and called it luck with their mouths. The wall called it **X-in-circle** with chalk and didn’t care who heard.\n\nTiffani rolled her thumb and let the pain have one breath of acknowledgment. The ache was cold enough to make her bite her lip. She logged it. She did not let it write anything else.\n\n“Again down-line,” the captain said. “Fools in the back still think this is theater.”\n\n“It’s not,” Jhace said, without heat. “Theater has better benches.”\n\n“Less soup,” Bram said.\n\nThey went down-line and did it again, then again. By the time the crowd understood the marks and the bells and the stopping, the rib had shifted from spectacle toward habit. Habit holds. That was the point.\n\nThey stood together at the crest, where Heat-Rib 3 showed its back to the sky. The hum under it changed, not loud, not polite—like a weight picking a new place to live. Flags cracked loose from themselves and remembered their jobs. The bell over Outer Crown found a pattern Tiffani didn’t like: short, short, long, hold.\n\n“New problem,” the captain said, squinting toward West-One.\n\n“Or old problem louder,” Bram said.\n\nTiffani flexed her useless fingers and let the reality exist: she would not be the hands for the next part. She looked at Jhace. He rolled his sleeve and checked the line and put the fabric back down. They had a page for **Stopping**; they had breath; they had the grammar they had taught into the crowd. Enough to begin.\n\n“Ready?” she asked him, because ridiculous questions are also measurements.\n\n“Ready,” he said.\n\nThey turned to face the next hinge. The crowd followed the motion with their attention like metal follows a magnet. Heat-Rib 3 waited with the patience of a large animal. The bell lied about rest. They pretended to believe it for a breath, then didn’t."
          ]
        },

    "115609b5-d194-4163-8bc3-ce0b5f8a358b": {
      "children": [
        "ce16cc8c-c547-4d9b-9638-92b223b37165"
      ],
      "id": "115609b5-d194-4163-8bc3-ce0b5f8a358b",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "commentary",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "# We'll attempt to open the uploaded DOCX, make targeted edits per the revision plan,\r\n# and save both a revised DOCX and a Markdown export for transparency.\r\n#\r\n# If python-docx isn't available, we'll gracefully fall back to creating a Markdown\r\n# \"patch\" and a compiled MD with best-effort text extraction.\r\n\r\nimport re\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\n\r\ninput_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds.docx\")\r\nrevised_docx_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REVISED.docx\")\r\nrevised_md_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REVISED.md\")\r\npatch_md_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Revision-Patch.md\")\r\n\r\n# Helper: safe import of python-docx\r\ntry:\r\n    from docx import Document\r\n    from docx.text.run import Run\r\n    docx_available = True\r\nexcept Exception as e:\r\n    docx_available = False\r\n    docx_import_error = str(e)\r\n\r\nchanges_log = []\r\n\r\ndef apply_text_replacements(text):\r\n    \"\"\"Apply line-level replacements to raw text (for MD fallback or docx paragraph text).\"\"\"\r\n    # 1) CH1 \"old pennies\" -> \"coin held too long under tongue\" and remove meta line\r\n    # We'll handle both cases: with and without smart quotes; also the following \"Then laughed...\" sentence.\r\n    # Create patterns that span potential quotation styles.\r\n    patterns = [\r\n        # Replace the sentence and remove meta joke.\r\n        (r'It tastes wrong,” Jhace said\\. “Like old pennies\\.” Then laughed at himself because pennies weren’t a thing here, just a story someone’s grandfather had told about some other city, some other casting\\. He set the key down\\. “Next\\.”',\r\n         'It tasted wrong,” Jhace said. “Like a coin held too long under tongue.” He set the key down. “Next.”'),\r\n        (r'It tastes wrong,” Jhace said\\. “Like old pennies\\.” He set the key down\\. “Next\\.”',\r\n         'It tasted wrong,” Jhace said. “Like a coin held too long under tongue.” He set the key down. “Next.”'),\r\n        # Straight quotes variant\r\n        (r'It tastes wrong,\" Jhace said\\. \"Like old pennies\\.\" Then laughed at himself because pennies weren\\'t a thing here, just a story someone\\'s grandfather had told about some other city, some other casting\\. He set the key down\\. \"Next\\.?\"',\r\n         'It tasted wrong,\" Jhace said. \"Like a coin held too long under tongue.\" He set the key down. \"Next.\"'),\r\n        (r'It tastes wrong,\" Jhace said\\. \"Like old pennies\\.\" He set the key down\\. \"Next\\.?\"',\r\n         'It tasted wrong,\" Jhace said. \"Like a coin held too long under tongue.\" He set the key down. \"Next.\"'),\r\n    ]\r\n    for pat, rep in patterns:\r\n        new_text, n = re.subn(pat, rep, text)\r\n        if n:\r\n            text = new_text\r\n            changes_log.append(f\"Replaced CH1 pennies line ({n} occurrence).\")\r\n\r\n    return text\r\n\r\ndef insert_after_heading(paragraphs, heading_text, insert_sentences):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Insert grounding sentences just after the specified heading paragraph text.\r\n    Returns count of inserts.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    count = 0\r\n    for i, p in enumerate(paragraphs):\r\n        if heading_text in p.text:\r\n            # Find the next non-empty paragraph after heading\r\n            j = i + 1\r\n            while j < len(paragraphs) and not paragraphs[j].text.strip():\r\n                j += 1\r\n            # Insert after that paragraph\r\n            ins_index = j + 1 if j < len(paragraphs) else i + 1\r\n            for sent in reversed(insert_sentences):\r\n                para = paragraphs[0]._element.__class__  # placeholder keep for type hint\r\n            count += 1\r\n            # We can't directly insert with python-docx without creating new paragraphs via document object.\r\n            # We'll signal where to insert and handle after by marking with a token.\r\n            marker = f\"[[INSERT_AFTER_{heading_text.replace(':','').replace(' ','_')}]]\"\r\n            # If next paragraph exists, append marker; else create a new one with marker\r\n            if j < len(paragraphs):\r\n                paragraphs[j].text = paragraphs[j].text + \"\\n\" + marker\r\n            else:\r\n                paragraphs[i].text = paragraphs[i].text + \"\\n\" + marker\r\n    return count\r\n\r\ndef inject_simple_markers_to_text(text):\r\n    \"\"\"Inject grounding lines and added beats using markers and then expand them to sentences.\"\"\"\r\n    # Add markers after chapter headings (robust to \"Chapter 1\" or \"Chapter 1:\" etc.)\r\n    # We'll add unique markers and later replace them with the target sentences.\r\n    markers = {\r\n        \"Chapter 1:\": \"The Underface smelled like limestone and old sweat.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 2:\": \"Heat made the measurement frame slick under her palms.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 3:\": \"Heat from the purge made the air shimmer like glass warping.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 6:\": \"Chalk dust made the air taste like dry bone.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 7:\": \"Two hundred bodies and bad air pressed at street level.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 8:\": \"Ink and hot metal gave the forge a bitter, steady smell.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 9:\": \"The ink office was warmer than the bells said it should be.\",\r\n        \"Chapter 10:\": \"Night-shift cold moved in the joints.\",\r\n    }\r\n    for chap, sent in markers.items():\r\n        # Insert the sentence after the first blank line following the heading.\r\n        # We'll look for the heading, then two line breaks, then insert.\r\n        pat = rf\"({re.escape(chap)}[^\\n]*\\n)(\\n?)\"\r\n        text, n = re.subn(pat, rf\"\\1\\2{sent}\\n\\n\", text, count=1)\r\n        if n:\r\n            changes_log.append(f\"Inserted grounding sentence after {chap}\")\r\n\r\n    # PASS 3: Chloen agency additions\r\n    # Chapter 9: manual creation scene — add drawing contribution\r\n    ch9_add = (\r\n        \"Chloen took the chalk from his hand. \\\"I'm better at pictures,\\\" she said, and proved it. \"\r\n        \"Her drawings were clumsy but true—the kind of true that made crews remember a mark when the page was gone. \"\r\n        \"By late watch, she'd drawn three pages that looked like they'd been torn from the city's own ledger.\\n\\n\"\r\n    )\r\n    text, n = re.subn(r\"(Chapter 9:.*?\\n\\n)\", rf\"\\1{ch9_add}\", text, count=1, flags=re.DOTALL)\r\n    if n:\r\n        changes_log.append(\"Added Chloen agency beat to Chapter 9 (manual drawings).\")\r\n\r\n    # Chapter 14: public demonstration — distribution of manuals\r\n    ch14_add = (\r\n        \"At the crowd's edge, Chloen moved between clusters with a bundle of hand-copied manuals. \"\r\n        \"Her drawings—rough but honest—spread through hands faster than speech. \"\r\n        \"By late glow, three crews had manuals they'd copied themselves, her pictures multiplying like chalk marks on a hot grid.\\n\\n\"\r\n    )\r\n    text, n = re.subn(r\"(Chapter 14:.*?\\n)\", rf\"\\1{ch14_add}\", text, count=1)\r\n    if n:\r\n        changes_log.append(\"Added Chloen agency beat to Chapter 14 (manual distribution).\")\r\n\r\n    # PASS 2: Vein-burn progression in Ch12-15\r\n    inserts = {\r\n        \"Chapter 12:\": \"He checked the line before sleep. Four centimeters now—halfway to his hand. The discipline had cost him half a finger's width in a single day.\\n\",\r\n        \"Chapter 13:\": \"The copper-brown line sat at four centimeters, patient as law. It hadn't moved since yesterday. For now, stopping held.\\n\",\r\n        \"Chapter 14:\": \"He rolled his wrist and checked truth. Four and a quarter—the demonstration had taken more than he'd wanted to spend. He pulled the sleeve down and didn't make bargains with himself.\\n\",\r\n        \"Chapter 15:\": \"The line had stopped at four and a quarter centimeters. Not longer, but the copper-gold warmth in his chest sat thin as morning mist. He had coins left. Not many.\\n\",\r\n    }\r\n    for chap, sent in inserts.items():\r\n        # Append near the end of the chapter (before next heading or EOF). We'll insert after the first occurrence of the chapter heading's first paragraph block.\r\n        # For simplicity, add right after the heading line.\r\n        pat = rf\"({re.escape(chap)}[^\\n]*\\n)\"\r\n        text, n = re.subn(pat, rf\"\\1{sent}\", text, count=1)\r\n        if n:\r\n            changes_log.append(f\"Inserted vein-burn tracking line in {chap}\")\r\n    return text\r\n\r\n# Execution path\r\nif docx_available and input_path.exists():\r\n    doc = Document(str(input_path))\r\n    # Extract paragraphs to a single text for easier regex-based ops (since structure is narrative)\r\n    raw_paragraphs = [p.text for p in doc.paragraphs]\r\n    original_text = \"\\n\".join(raw_paragraphs)\r\n\r\n    # Apply replacements and injections\r\n    revised_text = apply_text_replacements(original_text)\r\n    revised_text = inject_simple_markers_to_text(revised_text)\r\n\r\n    # Now, rebuild a new DOCX from the revised_text, separating paragraphs on double newlines.\r\n    new_doc = Document()\r\n    for block in revised_text.split(\"\\n\\n\"):\r\n        new_doc.add_paragraph(block)\r\n\r\n    new_doc.save(str(revised_docx_path))\r\n\r\n    # Also save a Markdown export (simple text with blank-line paragraph breaks)\r\n    revised_md_path.write_text(revised_text, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\n    # Create a patch log\r\n    patch_report = \"# N01 Revision Patch (Applied)\\n\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(f\"- {c}\" for c in changes_log)\r\n    patch_md_path.write_text(patch_report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\n    print(\"Revision complete.\")\r\n    print(\"Files created:\")\r\n    print(f\"- Revised DOCX: {revised_docx_path}\")\r\n    print(f\"- Revised MD:   {revised_md_path}\")\r\n    print(f\"- Patch Log:    {patch_md_path}\")\r\n\r\nelse:\r\n    # Fallback: if we can't parse docx, create a patch instruction file only.\r\n    note = \"# N01 Revision Patch (Instructions)\\n\\n\"\r\n    if not input_path.exists():\r\n        note += f\"- ERROR: Input file not found at {input_path}\\n\"\r\n    if not docx_available:\r\n        note += f\"- ERROR: python-docx unavailable: {docx_import_error}\\n\"\r\n    note += (\r\n        \"\\n## Apply the following changes manually:\\n\"\r\n        \"1) Replace CH1 'old pennies' line with: \\\"It tasted wrong,\\\" Jhace said. \\\"Like a coin held too long under tongue.\\\" Remove the meta aside about pennies not existing.\\n\"\r\n        \"2) Insert grounding sentences after headings:\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch1: The Underface smelled like limestone and old sweat.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch2: Heat made the measurement frame slick under her palms.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch3: Heat from the purge made the air shimmer like glass warping.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch6: Chalk dust made the air taste like dry bone.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch7: Two hundred bodies and bad air pressed at street level.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch8: Ink and hot metal gave the forge a bitter, steady smell.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch9: The ink office was warmer than the bells said it should be.\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch10: Night-shift cold moved in the joints.\\n\"\r\n        \"3) Add Chloen agency beats:\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch9: Add drawing paragraph (see plan).\\n\"\r\n        \"   - Ch14: Add manual distribution paragraph (see plan).\\n\"\r\n        \"4) Vein-burn progression lines at the start of Ch12-15 as specified in the plan.\\n\"\r\n    )\r\n    patch_md_path.write_text(note, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n    print(\"Could not modify DOCX directly; wrote patch instructions instead.\")\r\n    print(f\"- Patch Instructions: {patch_md_path}\")"
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1763201243.796336,

    "11b14460-20a7-4800-9b3d-37d150a1d46b": {
      "children": [
        "ec4a36ba-9d32-49ba-b4e1-5887665d1d7d"
      ],
      "id": "11b14460-20a7-4800-9b3d-37d150a1d46b",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "user"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "# CONFLUENCE CHRONICLES\n\n## RELATIONSHIP & CHARACTER DYNAMICS MAP — N01 “THE GRIP THAT HOLDS”\n\nThis is a companion to the N01 story bible: it focuses only on **relationships, emotional dynamics, and how characters interact on the page**. It’s meant to guide scene-level writing (or a prose engine) toward character-rich choices without touching world meta beyond what’s already in canon.\n\nAll notes below are **diegetic**: they assume nobody knows anything except what the characters know in N01.\n\n---\n\n## I. CORE FOUR – INDIVIDUAL HEARTS\n\n### Jhace Torrins – “Warmth That Won’t Let Go”\n\n* **Role:** Heat-grid specialist, Wholeness affinity (mending, connecting).\n* **Inner motor:**\n\n  * Survivor’s guilt from his parents’ deaths in an infrastructure collapse.\n  * Refuses to disengage from any problem if someone might get hurt. Hyperresponsible as a religion.\n* **How he relates to people:**\n\n  * Protective, physically present, service-oriented.\n  * Shows love through **work**, not talk. Helps fix your stuff, then your ribs, then your city.\n  * Terrible at asking for help; decent at quietly accepting it when forced.\n* **Baseline relationships:**\n\n  * Bram = anchor / surrogate father.\n  * Chloen = apprentice / kid sister energy.\n  * Tiffani = the first person who meets him at full craft-level intensity instead of “good worker, weird guy”.\n\n---\n\n### Tiffani Merrow – “Precision With Trust Issues”\n\n* **Role:** Glass-guild–trained structural specialist, Form affinity (geometry, structural integrity).\n* **Inner motor:**\n\n  * Past catastrophic failure involving her teacher Marisse; her self-worth is tied to never messing up again.\n  * Keeps people at distance; lets numbers close instead.\n* **How she relates to people:**\n\n  * Respect is given for competence, not rank.\n  * Affection shows up as teaching, sharing tools, parallel work, extremely rare verbal praise.\n  * Emotionally cautious; vulnerability feels like a risk to be quantified.\n* **Baseline relationships:**\n\n  * Jhace = proof that partnership can be precise instead of chaotic.\n  * Chloen = too honest, too earnest, sneaks past her guard.\n  * Bram = solid, irritatingly perceptive, earns her respect by respecting craft instead of titles.\n\n---\n\n### Bram – “Old Brace That Still Holds”\n\n* **Role:** Senior maintenance man / mentor, unofficial foreman of the Underface crew.\n* **Inner motor:**\n\n  * Lived through earlier collapses; saw Jhace’s parents die.\n  * Determined to keep Jhace alive long enough to have more than just work.\n* **How he relates to people:**\n\n  * Teases instead of praises; praise is rationed like water.\n  * Protective of Chloen, proud and worried about Jhace, cautiously approving of Tiffani.\n* **Baseline relationships:**\n\n  * Jhace = son he won’t call “son.”\n  * Chloen = promising kid he refuses to coddle.\n  * Tiffani = “glass-house brain” whose precision he comes to rely on.\n\n---\n\n### Chloen – “Bright Wire Under Load”\n\n* **Role:** Jhace’s apprentice; quietly anomalous with access to multiple principle expressions (e.g., Purity + Form) even if nobody has language for that yet.\n* **Inner motor:**\n\n  * Hero-worships Jhace; wants to be useful, not burdensome.\n  * Optimistic, scared, stubborn.\n* **How she relates to people:**\n\n  * Mouthy with Bram, reverent with Jhace, inquisitive with Tiffani.\n  * Craves inclusion in “the real work.”\n* **Baseline relationships:**\n\n  * Jhace = hero / mentor.\n  * Bram = gruff uncle / boss.\n  * Tiffani = intimidating genius she desperately wants to impress.\n\n---\n\n## II. CENTRAL RELATIONSHIP – JHACE & TIFFANI\n\nThis is the axis the novella spins on.\n\n### Core Dynamic\n\nFrom the bible:\n\n> Two people who speak through their hands—one heals with warmth, one builds with precision—forge love through heat, pressure, and permanent change.\n\n* **Shared traits:**\n\n  * Hypercompetent, deeply lonely, craft as identity, survivor’s guilt.\n* **Opposites:**\n\n  * Jhace: instinct-first, emotional, “listen to what the metal needs.”\n  * Tiffani: system-first, analytical, “measure three times, cut once.”\n\n### Relationship Phases Across N01\n\nFrom the existing bible:\n\n1. **Recognition (Ch1–5)**\n\n   * Separate intros: both competent, both alone.\n   * Glass-house rescue + quench-hall = trauma-bond + mutual awe.\n   * Emotional state: respect, curiosity, guarded.\n\n2. **Partnership (Ch6–10)**\n\n   * Teaching phase (she systematizes his instincts; he softens her rigidity).\n   * Rhythm phase (they move in sync, anticipate each other’s choices).\n   * Emotional state: trust, reliance, unnamed intimacy.\n\n3. **Intimacy (Ch11–15)**\n\n   * Life-or-death crisis; near burnout.\n   * First kiss and “I love you” in the shadow of possible failure.\n   * Public demonstration: they present as one unit under pressure.\n   * Emotional state: committed partnership, fully in love.\n\n### Relational Ladder – What the Engine Should Track\n\nFor each chapter, track **what changes between them**:\n\n* **Do they see a new side of each other?**\n* **Do they take on more shared risk?**\n* **Do they move from assumption → trust → commitment?**\n\nExample progression:\n\n* Early (Ch3–5):\n\n  * She sees him put his bare hands in molten glass for her.\n  * He sees her triage a collapsing room with calm, precise orders.\n\n* Middle (Ch6–9):\n\n  * They learn to name his warmth and her geometry as parts of the same grammar.\n  * They begin predicting each other’s movements and choices (“You knew I’d shift left.” / “Your weight changes when you’re about to move.”).\n\n* Late (Ch10–15):\n\n  * They commit to stand under public load together.\n  * They say “Whatever happens, I love you” on the morning of a test that could kill them both.\n\n---\n\n## III. THE INNER TRIANGLE – JHACE, BRAM, CHLOEN\n\n### Jhace & Bram – “Work as Parenting”\n\n* **Function:** mentor → father-figure.\n* **Baseline dynamic:**\n\n  * Bram calls out Jhace’s workaholism (“You’re good with metal. Less good with people.”).\n  * Jhace respects Bram’s judgment more than any authority figure.\n* **Key beats:**\n\n  * Ch1–2: Teaching Chloen + banter = established long history.\n  * Ch4–5: Bram supports Jhace’s decisions in the glass-house rescue + quench-hall, but also watches his hands and cost.\n  * Mid-book: Bram quietly approves of the partnership with Tiffani (“Parallel lines… working lines meet at the job”).\n* **Exit state from N01:**\n  Bram is proud, believes this partnership is healthy, lives to see the first big public success.\n\n### Jhace & Chloen – “Hero and Apprentice”\n\n* **Function:** show Jhace’s nurturing side & the cost of leadership.\n* **Baseline dynamic:**\n\n  * She follows his lead almost without question.\n  * He trains her firmly but kindly; he saves her life early, creating a deep bond.\n* **Key beats:**\n\n  * Early: “Gloves, if you want to keep skin” – automatic care + gentle correction.\n  * Injury + rescue: she’s impaled; he and Tiffani carry her to the quench-hall.\n  * Later: she drags herself back into the field and demands to still be part of the work.\n* **Exit state from N01:**\n  Chloen is alive, more capable, completely devoted. The bible flags her death in N02 as a devastating turning point.\n\n### Bram & Chloen – “Old Brace, New Wire”\n\n* **Function:** demonstrate generational continuity of craft.\n* **Dynamic:**\n\n  * He treats her like a junior crew member, not a child, which is precisely what she wants.\n  * Still quietly protective (watching her limp, watching how she holds her side).\n\n---\n\n## IV. TIFFANI WITH THE REST OF THE CREW\n\n### Tiffani & Bram – “Mutual Respect, Different Vocabulary”\n\n* **Function:** show practical adults aligning around the work.\n* **Beats:**\n\n  * Glass-house: he trusts her assessment immediately; they move as one unit in the rescue.\n  * Field days: he sees her as the missing structural brain they didn’t know they needed.\n  * Forge nights: he’s the first to point out she’s actually thinking about scale and crowds, not just local fixes (see sample scene below).\n\n### Tiffani & Chloen – “Sharp Edge Meets Bright Hope”\n\n* **Function:** open a softer side in Tiffani and give Chloen another role model.\n* **Beats:**\n\n  * Chloen blurts the truth (“You two work like you’re married”), forcing Tiffani to react.\n  * Manual drafting: Chloen takes the chalk from Jhace and proves she’s better at pictures; Tiffani recognizes the value and lets her own control loosen enough to share authorship.\n\n---\n\n## V. RELATIONSHIPS TO PEOPLE WHO AREN’T IN THE ROOM\n\nThese are mostly for **internal motivation**, not direct on-page interactions in N01.\n\n### Jhace & His Parents (Memory)\n\n* **Role:** emotional backstory.\n* **Effect on relationships:**\n\n  * Drives his refusal to step away from risk.\n  * Makes him extremely sensitive to any situation where people rely on him holding the line.\n\n### Tiffani & Marisse (Off-page Mentor)\n\n* **Role:** her formative failure.\n* **Effect:**\n\n  * She associates closeness with the possibility of catastrophic failure and rejection.\n  * Her arc with Jhace is partly about testing whether partnership can coexist with that fear.\n\n---\n\n## VI. QUIET MOMENTS & CONVERSATION SLOTS\n\nThese are **pre-approved places** where full conversations can live without derailing the plot. They should feel like breathers built from work, food, bandaging, or shared watch.\n\n### Slot A – Forge Night After “City Grammar”\n\n*(Post–Chapter 8 field work; all four together in Bram’s forge.)*\n\n**Purpose:**\n\n* Let them talk about:\n\n  * Why the manual exists.\n  * How each of them sees “the work.”\n  * What they think this grammar might do to the city.\n* Deepen:\n\n  * Jhace ↔ Tiffani (ideas about scale & public proof).\n  * Bram ↔ both (pride + caution).\n  * Chloen ↔ all three (kid who gets taken seriously).\n\n**Sample scene (drop-in ready):**\n\n> “It’s not a language,” Tiffani said. “It’s just a way to stop idiots from dying the same way twice.”\n>\n> Chloen, half-asleep on her folded arms, made a sound that might have been a laugh. “That’s all language is.”\n>\n> Bram tipped his cup toward the chalked floor-map. “Looks like a language. Acts like one. People start copying it, you don’t get to decide what it *is* anymore.”\n>\n> That pricked something in her; she covered it by nudging a chalk fragment with her boot until it snapped. “Copying is the point,” she said. “Otherwise it stays in my head and his hands.” She jerked her chin at Jhace. “Not useful.”\n>\n> Jhace leaned in the doorway, shoulders pressed to old wood like it was doing him a favor. “Some things work better quiet,” he said. “One brace, one fix, nobody watching. Manual feels loud.”\n>\n> “Loud is how you reach the ones who don’t listen,” Tiffani said. She made herself soften it. “You said yourself the city’s too big to fix brace by brace. So we teach them to hear what you hear. With chalk. With marks. With rules they can’t misinterpret if they squint.”\n>\n> “People can misinterpret anything,” Bram said. “Give them a straight line, they’ll find a way to bend it.”\n>\n> “Then we design the marks so bending hurts less than following them.”\n>\n> Chloen cracked one eye. “You’re talking like the whole city’s in the room.”\n>\n> “It will be,” Tiffani said, before she could stop herself.\n>\n> Jhace’s gaze snapped from the map to her. “What?”\n>\n> She rolled one shoulder, casual like a badly-fitted coat. “Not literally. But if this works, it won’t stay in this forge. We’re not scratching a private code to make us feel clever. We’re building something we can put in front of captains and wall-runners and whoever else insists they already know which ribs will hold.”\n>\n> Bram huffed. “You planning to drag the Council in here and make them sit on the floor?”\n>\n> “They won’t come here,” she said. “But they don’t have to. We bring the marks to them. To the braces they stand under.”\n>\n> Jhace chewed that for a few heartbeats. “I fix metal,” he said finally. “I don’t perform for crowds.”\n>\n> “You performed today,” Chloen muttered. “Whole street saw you talk a hinge into behaving. You going to pretend that didn’t happen?”\n>\n> “That was just work,” he said.\n>\n> “Work can be a show,” Tiffani said. “Sometimes it has to be. People trust what they see more than what they read. If they watch a brace listen to you? They’ll listen to the chalk too.”\n>\n> Bram squinted at her. “You’ve thought about this.”\n>\n> “I think about scale,” she said. “If an idea can’t hold at scale, it’s a toy. This isn’t a toy.”\n>\n> Jhace watched his own hands a second too long. “If we do this,” he said, “we do it right. No half-measures.”\n>\n> “Agreed,” Tiffani said. “No lies. Clean lines. Clean tests.”\n>\n> Bram lifted his cup. “To clean tests,” he said. “And to whoever has to stand under the first one.”\n>\n> Chloen bumped her mug against his. “We will,” she said. “If we’re asking anyone else to.”\n>\n> Jhace let his head fall back against the doorframe for one indulgent breath, listening: forge settling, Chloen’s slow respiration, Tiffani’s measured count of inhale-exhale, the city’s third note under it all. A roomful of noises he wasn’t ready to lose.\n>\n> “Copy,” he said.\n\n---\n\n### Slot B – Quench-Hall, Before Copper-Gold\n\n*(Chapter 5, after the quench-sister gives them “half a watch.”)*\n\n**Purpose:**\n\n* Put Jhace, Bram, and Tiffani in direct conflict over cost.\n* Clarify how each thinks about **who is worth spending what**.\n\n**Sample scene:**\n\n> The quench-sister moved on, hands already bloody in three directions. The air held the shape of her absence like a missing brace.\n>\n> “Half a watch,” Bram said. His voice had that too-even quality he got when something inside had already broken. “That’s generous.”\n>\n> Chloen made a small sound. Not quite a word, not quite a sob.\n>\n> Jhace’s hands flexed uselessly at his sides. “There has to be more we can do.”\n>\n> “Than sit and watch?” Bram cut in. “Sometimes that’s the whole job, boy.”\n>\n> Tiffani watched Jhace’s shoulders bunch, like a beam loading past its rating.\n>\n> “No,” Jhace said. “Not this time.” He looked at the copper line on his wrist, then at Chloen, then at the maze of cots. “Not when I can hear it.”\n>\n> “Hear what?” Bram demanded.\n>\n> “Her. Slipping.”\n>\n> Tiffani believed him. That wasn’t the point. “Even if you can,” she said carefully, “what do you think you’ll do about it? You’re not a quench-sister. You’re not Soulhouse.”\n>\n> He flinched at the last word. “I’m the one who dragged her into this,” he said. “My marks. My brace. My idea. I don’t get to stand here and let her bleed out because someone with a badge said ‘wait.’”\n>\n> Bram rounded on him. “You going to fix her the way you fixed that hinge? With your bare hands and whatever that copper trick is? You don’t even know what it’s doing to you.”\n>\n> “I know what not doing it will do to her,” Jhace snapped.\n>\n> Tiffani lifted a hand. “Stop. Both of you.” She stepped into the narrow space between them. “We don’t have time for this. We have time to make one decision and live with it.”\n>\n> “Easy for you to say,” Bram said. “It’s not your skin on the line.”\n>\n> “He already put his hands in molten glass for a stranger,” she said. “Did you really think he’d stand back for Chloen?”\n>\n> Bram’s face twisted. “That’s exactly why someone has to tell him no.”\n>\n> “Then say it plain,” Tiffani said. “Tell him you’d rather bury her than risk losing him.”\n>\n> The silence that followed was heavier than any brace.\n>\n> Bram looked at Chloen, at Jhace, back again. “I don’t want to bury either of you,” he said. “That’s the problem.”\n>\n> “There isn’t a path where nobody pays,” Tiffani said, soft but sharp. “There’s only a choice about who and for what.”\n>\n> Jhace met her eyes. “If it were you on the cot,” he said, “what would you want me to do?”\n>\n> “I’d want you to do what only you can,” she said. “Because anything less would be an insult.”\n>\n> Bram swore. “You’re both damn fools.”\n>\n> “Probably,” she said. “But only one of us can reach in there.” She nodded at Chloen’s shaking chest. “Only one of us hears the wrongness like a bent beam. That makes it his decision. Not yours. Not mine.”\n>\n> Jhace swallowed. “If I do this and it goes bad—”\n>\n> “Then you’ll have tried,” she said. “And I’ll live with the cost. So will Bram. So will the city, if we’re lucky enough to still have one.”\n>\n> She watched his posture shift from flailing to grim alignment.\n>\n> “All right,” Jhace said quietly. “Move the rail. I need both hands.”\n\n---\n\n### Slot C – Night Before the Public Demonstration\n\n*(Between Ch14 and Ch15; Jhace & Tiffani alone.)*\n\n**Purpose:**\n\n* Let them talk explicitly about:\n\n  * Fear\n  * Public vs private work\n  * Why they’re choosing to stand under the rib together\n\n**Sample scene:**\n\n> The city hadn’t cooled. It had just gone quiet enough that every creak sounded like a judgment.\n>\n> Jhace sat on the forge steps, elbows on his knees, watching breath fog and vanish. The furnace slept behind him, coals banked under ash.\n>\n> Tiffani’s boots were softer on the stone than they had any right to be. He only knew she was there when her shadow cut across his.\n>\n> “You should be asleep,” she said.\n>\n> “You should be yelling at me about tomorrow’s sequence,” he said.\n>\n> “I finished yelling two tides ago,” she said. “You didn’t hear because you were busy pretending you can’t read when your name’s on a schedule.”\n>\n> He huffed. “I can read. I just don’t like seeing ‘stand directly under experimental load’ in ink.”\n>\n> She sat beside him, not touching, close enough that he felt her warmth. “No one wrote that part,” she said. “You volunteered it.”\n>\n> “Did I?” he asked. “Feels like I blinked and suddenly there was a crowd in the picture.”\n>\n> “You were the one who insisted the marks had to work in front of anyone,” she said. “Remember? ‘If it only holds when it’s just us, it’s a trick, not a fix.’”\n>\n> He grimaced. “I should stop saying things that can be used against me.”\n>\n> “Too late,” she said. “I filed that under ‘useful.’”\n>\n> They let the quiet sit. The city made old-man noises in the dark.\n>\n> “You scared?” she asked.\n>\n> “Of the rib failing? Always,” he said. “Of doing it in front of half the Crown?” His mouth twisted. “More.”\n>\n> “Because they’ll watch you.”\n>\n> “Because they’ll watch her,” he said. “The city. If we mess this up, they’ll blame the wrong things.”\n>\n> “So don’t mess it up,” she said. “Do what you always do. Listen. Adjust. Hold.”\n>\n> “You make it sound simple,” he said.\n>\n> “It is simple,” she said. “Not safe. Just simple.”\n>\n> “What’s the outcome you want?” he asked.\n>\n> “Rib holds,” she said. “Crowd goes home bored. Manual gets stamped by someone who doesn’t understand half of it and signs anyway because the numbers say ‘fewer dead.’ You and I get to argue for the rest of our lives about whether we should’ve done it sooner.”\n>\n> He let out a breath that misted and vanished. “Could live with that.”\n>\n> “Good,” she said. “So tomorrow when your knees go soft and your hands remember the glass, you can remind yourself you already decided.”\n>\n> “You think my knees are going to go soft?”\n>\n> “I think you’re human,” she said. “Mostly.”\n>\n> He watched his scarred hands. “If the rib starts to go, and it’s you or them,” he said quietly, “you know which way I’m tipping.”\n>\n> “I do,” she said. “I’m counting on you doing what only you can.”\n>\n> He was quiet a long time. “You scared?” he asked.\n>\n> She considered lying. “Yes,” she said. “But not of the rib.”\n>\n> “Of what, then?”\n>\n> “Of what happens if we prove this works,” she said. “People get very brave when they think the world is safer than it is.”\n>\n> “We’ll deal with that when it comes,” he said.\n>\n> “Yes,” she said. “We will.”\n\n---\n\n## VII. HOW TO USE THIS MAP\n\nFor each chapter or scene the prose engine generates, it should:\n\n* Check **which relationship phase** each pair is in (Recognition / Partnership / Intimacy).\n* Choose **one small relational change** to implement:\n\n  * A new detail revealed.\n  * A new trust test passed or failed.\n  * A small shift in how they stand, speak, or share tools.\n* Optionally grab one of the **quiet slots** and expand it into a full page of dialogue built around:\n\n  * Work they’re doing.\n  * Who pays which cost.\n  * What they’re afraid to lose.\n\nYou already have the infrastructure bible and the magic bible. This is the **people bible**: who stands where when the world starts to shift, and how that standing changes over time."
          ]

    "16d38b8a-279c-4904-941d-e9a07ca35d88": {
      "children": [
        "b28da88c-68cf-4250-a705-05da2deeabb8"
      ],
      "id": "16d38b8a-279c-4904-941d-e9a07ca35d88",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "commentary",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "# Re-run the revision with a clean session.\r\n\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nimport re\r\n\r\nfrom docx import Document\r\n\r\nrev_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REVISED.docx\")\r\nassert rev_path.exists(), \"Revised DOCX not found; please ensure the previous revision file exists at /mnt/data.\"\r\n\r\ndoc = Document(str(rev_path))\r\nparas = [p.text for p in doc.paragraphs]\r\ntext = \"\\n\".join(paras)\r\n\r\nchanges = []\r\n\r\n# ---- 1) Insert Corlexi horror passage before Part B ----\r\ncorlexi_insert = (\r\n    \"Meve will still be useful, he reminded himself, and looked for the fault he could remove.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Adren’s hands shook as he lifted the next length. The tremor traveled into the truss and made a small, ragged song.\\n\"\r\n    \"“Still,” Corlexi said. The word did not arrive as instruction. It arrived as fact.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Blue-silver gathered across his knuckles, thin as wire. He pressed two fingers to Adren’s forearm and drew a line the way he drew it in steel—along grain, not across it. The boy’s tremor flattened. The arm held with a borrowed geometry that did not belong to meat.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Adren sucked a breath. “Master, that hurts.”\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"“Pain is a measurement,” Corlexi said. “It tells us where the slack was.”\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Meve watched and did not speak. The lattice sang cleaner. The crew stood taller as if someone had subtracted weight from the day and added it somewhere else.\\n\\n\"\r\n    \"Adren tried to flex. The wrist did not answer like a wrist. It answered like a brace. Corlexi approved. The work would hold better now.\\n\\n\"\r\n)\r\n\r\npattern_b = r\"(PART B:\\s*MOBEL\\s*—\\s*CLEANs?ING FIRE)\"\r\nif re.search(pattern_b, text, flags=re.IGNORECASE):\r\n    text = re.sub(pattern_b, corlexi_insert + r\"\\1\", text, count=1, flags=re.IGNORECASE)\r\n    changes.append(\"Inserted Corlexi body-horror passage before PART B.\")\r\n\r\n# ---- 2) Fix grounding lines for 7-10 ----\r\nreplacements = [\r\n    (r\"(Chapter 7:[^\\n]*\\n)(Two hundred bodies and bad air pressed at street level\\.)\",\r\n     r\"\\1Their breath made small clouds in the morning cold.\"),\r\n    (r\"(Chapter 8:[^\\n]*\\n)(Ink and hot metal gave the forge a bitter, steady smell\\.)\",\r\n     r\"\\1Two hundred bodies made the rib warmer than the bells said it should be.\"),\r\n    (r\"(Chapter 9:[^\\n]*\\n)(The ink office was warmer than the bells said it should be\\.)\",\r\n     r\"\\1Ink stained his fingers the color of old bruises.\"),\r\n    (r\"(Chapter 10:[^\\n]*\\n)(Night-shift cold moved in the joints\\.)\",\r\n     r\"\\1Night-shift cold made his scars ache.\")\r\n]\r\n\r\nfor pat, rep in replacements:\r\n    new_text, n = re.subn(pat, rep, text, flags=re.DOTALL)\r\n    if n:\r\n        text = new_text\r\n        changes.append(f\"Adjusted grounding line with pattern: {pat[:30]}...\")\r\n\r\n# Ensure Chapter 9 ink-grounding exists\r\nif not re.search(r\"Chapter 9:[^\\n]*\\n.*Ink stained his fingers the color of old bruises\\.\", text, flags=re.DOTALL):\r\n    text = re.sub(r\"(Chapter 9:[^\\n]*\\n)\", r\"\\1Ink stained his fingers the color of old bruises.\\n\\n\", text, count=1, flags=re.DOTALL)\r\n    changes.append(\"Inserted missing Chapter 9 grounding line at opening.\")\r\n\r\n# ---- Write DOCX and MD ----\r\nfrom docx import Document as Doc2\r\nnew_doc = Doc2()\r\nfor line in text.split(\"\\n\"):\r\n    new_doc.add_paragraph(line)\r\nout_path = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REVISED2.docx\")\r\nnew_doc.save(str(out_path))\r\n\r\nmd_out = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01-Grip-That-Holds-REVISED2.md\")\r\nmd_out.write_text(text, encoding=\"utf-8\")\r\n\r\nprint(\"Applied changes:\")\r\nfor c in changes:\r\n    print(\"-\", c)\r\nprint(\"Saved:\", out_path)\r\nprint(\"Saved:\", md_out)"
          ]
        },
